_comment: Provenance + reproducibility manifest for the toolkit's REAL data sources. IMPORTANT: nothing here ships in the repo — all of data/, outputs/ and _tmp_fetch/ are gitignored. 'local_file' is where an extract lives AFTER you build it locally (see data/README.md); it is NOT committed. derived_extract entries carry a sha256/rows so verify_data.py can detect drift in your local build. Large raw sources and external caches (_tmp_fetch/*) are documented but not hashed.

eve_cftr.license: EVE / evemodel.org terms — CONFIRM before publishing this extract

gnomad_missense.query: gene(gene_id: ENSG00000001626, reference_genome: GRCh38){ variants(dataset: gnomad_r4){ variant_id hgvsc hgvsp consequence exome{ac an af} genome{ac an af} } }
gnomad_missense.note: NO PASS/AC filter applied -> 2466 incl. AC0/VQSR-filtered; browser PASS+AC>0 = 2133 (see plan F1).

alphamissense.version: AlphaMissense (Cheng et al. 2023, PMID 37733863); filtered to UniProt P13569

clinvar.version: ClinVar release date UNPINNED — record the exact release (plan R28)
clinvar.note: ClinVar updates ~weekly; the pinned release drives the A1 VUS set.

cadd.version: CADD v1.7 (GRCh38); Rentzsch et al. 2021, PMID 33618777
cadd.note: Queried live per-variant; cache responses to make reproducible (plan R41).

spliceai.source: Illumina BaseSpace share https://basespace.illumina.com/s/otSPW8hnhaZR ('Predicting splicing from primary sequence' / genome_scores_v1.3)
spliceai.version: SpliceAI precomputed v1.3, GRCh38, masked SNV; Gencode v24, distance 50bp
spliceai.license: CC BY-NC 4.0 (Jaganathan et al. 2019, PMID 30661751) — attribute SpliceAI+Illumina; do NOT commit raw VCF; publishing derived extract needs NC notice (plan R43)

esm1b.version: ESM1b LLR, canonical UniProt P13569 (Brandes et al. 2023, PMID 37563329)
esm1b.license: see ntranoslab esm-variants repo (MIT code); scores per publication

revel.version: REVEL v1.3, GRCh38 (Ioannidis et al. 2016, PMID 27666373)
revel.license: free for NON-COMMERCIAL use (contact authors otherwise)

primateai.version: PrimateAI (Sundaram et al. 2018, PMID 30038395) via dbNSFP; ClinVar-subset (~1,976, NOT saturation)
